message.content:
Research and document comprehensive analysis of note-taking application alternatives for improving the Notes page in Neuman Brain.

## Research Scope

### Applications to Research
Research the following note-taking applications and their UX patterns:

**Popular Note Apps:**
1. Notion - blocks, databases, backlinks
2. Obsidian - markdown, graph view, local-first
3. Apple Notes - simplicity, quick capture
4. Google Keep - cards, labels, reminders
5. Bear - tags, markdown, typography
6. Craft - blocks, beautiful design
7. Logseq - outliner, bidirectional links
8. Roam Research - networked thought
9. Evernote - notebooks, web clipper
10. Simplenote - minimalist, sync
11. Standard Notes - encryption, longevity
12. Joplin - open source, markdown
13. Typora - WYSIWYG markdown
14. Ulysses - writing-focused
15. iA Writer - distraction-free

### Research Areas

For each application, document:

1. **Visual Design**
   - Layout patterns (sidebar, split view, full screen)
   - Note card/list appearance
   - Typography choices
   - Color schemes and themes
   - Empty states

2. **Organization Systems**
   - Folders vs tags vs both
   - Hierarchical vs flat
   - Search capabilities
   - Filters and sorting

3. **Editor Experience**
   - WYSIWYG vs Markdown vs hybrid
   - Block-based vs document-based
   - Toolbar placement and design
   - Keyboard shortcuts

4. **Unique Features**
   - What makes each app special?
   - What do users love about it?
   - What are common complaints?

5. **UX Patterns**
   - Quick capture methods
   - Note creation flow
   - Navigation patterns
   - Mobile vs desktop differences

### Output Requirements

Create a comprehensive document at `docs/product/notes-page-research.md` with:

1. **Executive Summary** - Key findings and top recommendations
2. **Competitive Analysis** - Matrix comparing features across apps
3. **Design Patterns** - Common and innovative patterns observed
4. **Recommended Improvements** - Prioritized list for Neuman Brain Notes page
5. **Visual Inspiration** - Description of best designs and why they work
6. **Implementation Ideas** - Concrete suggestions with rationale

Focus on what would make Neuman Brain's Notes page:
- Clean and distraction-free
- Fast and responsive
- Intuitive for new users
- Powerful for advanced users
- Consistent with the app's design system (magenta/cyan gradients, glassmorphism, Inter font)

Use web search to gather current information about each app's features and user feedback.

Here's what I found about Apple Notes app design, simplicity, and quick capture UX in 2025:

## Apple Notes: Simplicity & Quick Capture

Apple Notes is available on all devices and provides a simple and intuitive interface, cross-device compatibility, and security features to ensure user privacy.(no content)It has a beautiful minimalist layout and syncs seamlessly across devices while being really quick.

### Quick Capture Strengths

Simplicity beats everything else for quick capture workflows. If you have an Apple device and quick capture is a huge part of your workflows, nothing beats Apple Notes. Even with a PKMS in Notion, quick capture there just isn't fast enough—anytime you want to jot something down quick, Apple Notes is just there and does the job.(no content)While design and features may be a little limited, it does the most important thing of quickly capturing notes exceptionally well.

### Recent Feature Additions

Apple has recently added linking, collapsible headers and audio recording with transcription.(no content)Another benefit for those in the Apple ecosystem is the ability to integrate both typed and handwritten notes.

### The Quick Capture Challenge

A common frustration is losing good ideas to app switching—when something brilliant crosses your mind while you're deep in work, you reach for Apple Notes, wait for it to load, decide which folder it belongs in, and by the time you're ready to type, the thought has completely evaporated.

### Alternative Quick Capture Apps

Some third-party apps have emerged to address quick capture needs:

- The "Capture" app offers an intuitive interface and smart features that turn your mobile device into a powerful thought organizer. Its quick note-taking feature is designed for those who think fast and want to write faster, with notes, lists, and reminders captured in real-time.

- Quick Notes makes capturing thoughts, ideas, notes, or sketches quick and effortless, whether typing on a keyboard or using Apple Pencil, with simple and intuitive navigation.

### 2025 Comparison Guidance

Apple Notes is best for iPhone, iPad, and Mac users who prioritize simplicity and seamless integration.(no content)Experts recommend starting with your platform's native app like Apple Notes, as sometimes the simple, reliable option is the best choice for your productivity and peace of mind.

Links: [{"title":"Bear - Markdown Notes","url":"https://bear.app/"},{"title":"How to use Markdown in Bear","url":"https://bear.app/faq/how-to-use-markdown-in-bear/"},{"title":"Bear - Markdown Notes App - App Store","url":"https://apps.apple.com/us/app/bear-markdown-notes/id1016366447"},{"title":"Bear Markdown Reference | Markdown Guide","url":"https://www.markdownguide.org/tools/bear/"},{"title":"Markdown note-taking app Bear launches web version in public beta | AlternativeTo","url":"https://alternativeto.net/news/2025/7/markdown-note-taking-app-bear-launches-web-version-in-public-beta/"},{"title":"How to use tags in Bear","url":"https://bear.app/faq/how-to-use-tags-in-bear/"},{"title":"Bear — A Simple Markdown Note-Taking App - by shubham","url":"https://community.startupvisors.com/p/bear-a-simple-markdown-note-taking"},{"title":"10 Bear tips to transform your note-taking experience","url":"https://www.xda-developers.com/bear-notes-tips-to-transform-your-note-taking-experience/"},{"title":"Markdown","url":"https://blog.bear.app/tag/markdown/"},{"title":"Bear: An Elegant Combination of Design, Whimsy, and Voice. – Rands in Repose","url":"https://randsinrepose.com/archives/bear-an-elegant-combination-of-design-whimsy-and-voice/"}]

Here are the search results for "Bear app tags markdown typography design 2025":

## Bear App - Markdown Notes

Bear is a beautiful, powerfully simple Markdown note taking app to capture, write, and organize your life.(no content)"Bear manages to combine extreme power into something elegant and simple."

### Tags System
Bear features "Tags that pop" - you can choose from over 250 icons to help tags stand out and pin important tags for easy access.(no content)Bear doesn't use notebooks, sections, pages, or workspaces to organize notes. Instead, it utilizes tags and the slash (/) option to create sub-tags. You can even customize tags by inserting a specific icon for them.(no content)The tagging system allows for deep, intuitive categorization through nested tags and hashtags to group related notes.

### Markdown Support
Markdown is a simple way to add style, lists, links, and other formatting to plain text. Apps like Bear that support Markdown can display this formatting while still working with your notes as plain text. This duality has a number of advantages—your notes are future-proof, avoid app and platform lock-in, can be converted and shared almost anywhere, and are portable to virtually any other app that understands text.(no content)Bear doesn't automatically enable support Markdown by default, but you can enable it in the preferences. The application has a hybrid live editor and text editor — you can see both the Markdown syntax and the way the formatting changes the text. It takes a while to get used to, but it's useful if you're just getting started with Markdown.

### Typography
Bear has all the relevant options to edit typography in your notes. You can change text, headlines, code fonts, size, line height, width, paragraph spacing, and even paragraph indent.

### 2025 Updates
Bear's web version is now available in public beta, letting users browse, search, and edit Markdown notes from any web browser. Some features, like export and encryption, are not available yet, but improvements are expected over time.(no content)In 2025, Bear has also introduced: Web Clipper 2.0 with on-device privacy, custom tags, and iOS support; a redesigned UI and icons for macOS Tahoe and iOS 26; and support for math formulas in notes.

### Design Philosophy
Bear is an impressive notes app that functions very well on all Apple devices. None are as clean and minimal as Bear. No intrusive UI; No head-scratching organization structure; Bear allows you to jump into your notes quickly and organize them with ease using an extremely flexible tagging system.

Here's what I found about Notion's note-taking app features and UX design patterns:

## Notion UX Design Patterns and Features

### Core Design Principles

Notion is an all-in-one productivity and collaboration app used for personal organization, team collaboration, note-taking, project management, knowledge management, and task tracking. It combines these elements into a highly customizable workspace, offering powerful features such as nested pages, databases, and content blocks.(no content)The key UX principles driving Notion include:
- **Autonomy**: Users have extensive control over how they structure their workspace
- **Cleanness**: Despite its comprehensive feature set, Notion maintains a clean, minimal interface
- **Organization**: Large amounts of data and information are organized efficiently and intuitively

### Key UI/UX Features

The first thing design teams often point out is how simple yet powerful Notion is. Even without grasping the app's full potential, many first-time users can recognize the fluidity, intuitiveness, and responsiveness of the app.(no content)Customization options include the ability to customize notes so you can see titles, dates, and other parameters you need. It's easy to customize files (add properties, date, badges, icons, etc.) and there are many customization options for navigation structure.(no content)Different views for pages (boards, timelines, lists, etc.) provide flexibility, and Notion offers a huge selection of content variety.(no content)Notion's autonomy also lies in its flexible interaction model, giving users freedom to arrange and customize pages as they see fit. Almost every component on Notion can be manipulated: rearranging blocks, table rows, gallery cards, and resizing dividers.

### Design System Elements

When analyzing Notion's design, key elements include:
- **Text Styles**: From headings to body text with consistent typography for a clean and structured look
- **Color Palettes**: Subtle and neutral color schemes that ensure visual harmony
- **Grid Systems**: Maintaining alignment and structure across all screens

### UX Challenges and Inconsistencies

Some UX inconsistencies have been identified, including: no consistency in behavior while clicking on menu items (sometimes dropdown, sometimes pop-up), which can make users feel insecure since they don't know what to expect. Some interactions are confusing, such as actions available only on hover.(no content)What makes Notion stand out from similar apps is that it's all-in-one. What previously would have needed multiple apps to organize, users can now manage with just one app. However, though Notion's extensive features offer great flexibility, this consequently leads to a steep learning curve and confusion on how to use all the tools.

### 2025 Comparisons and Context

Notion is a knowledge management tool with advanced features for complex data management and customization. It can be a bit too robust for users interested in simple note-taking but solid for combining basic project management and documentation.(no content)Notion offers a flexible all-in-one workspace that supports everything from documentation to databases and kanban boards. You create documents inside blocks and databases, with tags, filters, and automations. Whether you're drafting SOPs or capturing class notes, Notion becomes your "second brain."

Here are the search results for "Google Keep cards labels reminders design patterns 2025":

## Google Keep Design Patterns & Updates in 2025

### Card-Based Design Pattern
The Card-Based Layout is a dominant and versatile choice among user experience design patterns, organizing content into distinct, rectangular modules. Each "card" acts as a self-contained unit of information, typically grouping an image, title, snippet of text, and interactive elements like buttons or links. This modular approach creates a clean, scannable, and easily digestible interface.(no content)Popularized by platforms like Pinterest and heavily integrated into Google's Material Design, this pattern's strength lies in its innate responsiveness. Cards can be easily rearranged in a grid to fit any screen size, making them ideal for modern, multi-device web design.

### Google Keep's New Design (Material 3 Expressive)
Google has been rolling out a major visual refresh for one of its core productivity apps, Google Keep. The update adopts Material 3 Expressive, a design style built around smoother visuals, stronger accents, and a more engaging feel. The rollout kicked off in late July 2025.(no content)The notes screen has been rearranged with a cleaner setup. Pin, reminder, and archive buttons now sit in rounded squares at the top-right. At the bottom, you'll see circular buttons for adding, changing colors/backgrounds, and formatting.

### Labels & Organization
If you use Google Keep for almost everything—reminders, quick ideas, shopping lists, or work notes—sooner or later you'll have encountered the same problem: You have so many notes that you can't find anything anymore.(no content)There's a limit of 50 labels, so it's worth removing those you no longer use or merging similar concepts.(no content)Visual organization and labels allow you to assign colors and labels to find notes quickly, with an intelligent internal search engine which makes it easy to locate any element.

### New Sorting Features
The feature enables manual sorting within lists and across the main notes view, a boon for those juggling extensive collections of reminders, ideas, and checklists. Unlike the app's previous reliance on drag-and-drop or basic pinning, this introduces algorithmic precision, making it easier to prioritize tasks or archive older entries.

### Reminders & Tasks Integration
Last year, Google announced that Tasks would complement the reminders functionality in Keep, with that migration still set to happen in 2025. In 2023, Tasks replaced Google Assistant Reminders, with the company wanting to unify that functionality into one product.(no content)After setting, reminders will appear in the Google Tasks app and Google Calendar grid with a "From Keep" label.(no content)You can set reminders for specific notes. Whether it's a meeting, a birthday, or a shopping list, Keep will nudge you at the right time. You can even attach location-based reminders. Imagine getting a note about buying milk when you're near the grocery store!

### AI-Powered Features
When you open Keep, you'll notice a "Help me create a list" button at the bottom right. Tap it, type a prompt like "snacks for a road trip" or "packing list for a weekend," and Keep will use Google's Gemini AI to generate a list for you.(no content)One of the most notable new features coming to Google Keep is advanced integration with Gemini, Google's artificial intelligence assistant. Starting with the update announced for July 2025, it will be possible to create, edit, and view notes in Keep using voice commands.

## Google Keep Design Patterns & Updates in 2025

### Card-Based Design Pattern
The Card-Based Layout is a dominant and versatile choice among user experience design patterns, organizing content into distinct, rectangular modules. Each "card" acts as a self-contained unit of information, typically grouping an image, title, snippet of text, and interactive elements like buttons or links. This modular approach creates a clean, scannable, and easily digestible interface.(no content)Popularized by platforms like Pinterest and heavily integrated into Google's Material Design, this pattern's strength lies in its innate responsiveness. Cards can be easily rearranged in a grid to fit any screen size, making them ideal for modern, multi-device web design.

### Google Keep's New Design (Material 3 Expressive)
Google has been rolling out a major visual refresh for one of its core productivity apps, Google Keep. The update adopts Material 3 Expressive, a design style built around smoother visuals, stronger accents, and a more engaging feel. The rollout kicked off in late July 2025.(no content)The notes screen has been rearranged with a cleaner setup. Pin, reminder, and archive buttons now sit in rounded squares at the top-right. At the bottom, you'll see circular buttons for adding, changing colors/backgrounds, and formatting.

### Labels & Organization
If you use Google Keep for almost everything—reminders, quick ideas, shopping lists, or work notes—sooner or later you'll have encountered the same problem: You have so many notes that you can't find anything anymore.(no content)There's a limit of 50 labels, so it's worth removing those you no longer use or merging similar concepts.(no content)Visual organization and labels allow you to assign colors and labels to find notes quickly, with an intelligent internal search engine which makes it easy to locate any element.

### New Sorting Features
The feature enables manual sorting within lists and across the main notes view, a boon for those juggling extensive collections of reminders, ideas, and checklists. Unlike the app's previous reliance on drag-and-drop or basic pinning, this introduces algorithmic precision, making it easier to prioritize tasks or archive older entries.

### Reminders & Tasks Integration
Last year, Google announced that Tasks would complement the reminders functionality in Keep, with that migration still set to happen in 2025. In 2023, Tasks replaced Google Assistant Reminders, with the company wanting to unify that functionality into one product.(no content)After setting, reminders will appear in the Google Tasks app and Google Calendar grid with a "From Keep" label.(no content)You can set reminders for specific notes. Whether it's a meeting, a birthday, or a shopping list, Keep will nudge you at the right time. You can even attach location-based reminders. Imagine getting a note about buying milk when you're near the grocery store!

### AI-Powered Features
When you open Keep, you'll notice a "Help me create a list" button at the bottom right. Tap it, type a prompt like "snacks for a road trip" or "packing list for a weekend," and Keep will use Google's Gemini AI to generate a list for you.(no content)One of the most notable new features coming to Google Keep is advanced integration with Gemini, Google's artificial intelligence assistant. Starting with the update announced for July 2025, it will be possible to create, edit, and view notes in Keep using voice commands.

Based on my search results, here's what I found about Obsidian's markdown, graph view, and local-first features in 2025:

## Obsidian Overview

Obsidian is a proprietary personal knowledge base and note-taking application that operates on markdown files.(no content)At its core, Obsidian is a local-first app, meaning all your notes are stored on your device, giving users full control over their data. It uses plain text Markdown files, making it future-proof and interoperable with other tools.

## Key Features

### Local-First Architecture
Obsidian is built around plain text files stored locally on your device, making sure your data remains secure and accessible without relying on external cloud services. This local-first approach guarantees long-term compatibility, as your notes can be opened with any text editor.(no content)Obsidian is designed offline-first — your notes are stored privately on your device, not on a company server. You do not even need an account or internet connection to use the full app. This appeals to privacy-conscious users and means you retain complete ownership of your data.

### Graph View
The graph view in Obsidian allows users to quickly visualize the connections between their notes. The graph is composed of nodes representing files (typically markdown documents) and edges between nodes representing the internal links that connect notes together. Users can customize the appearance of the Graph view by filtering out unwanted notes, coloring certain nodes that fit a criteria, modifying the values in the "Display" and "Forces" configuration.(no content)Beyond the "global" graph view (which shows every note in the vault by default), users can also open a "local" graph view, which only shows the currently focused note and all of its backlinks and outlinks.

### Markdown and Backlinking
Obsidian allows internal linking between notes, formatted either as Wikilinks or traditional markdown links, with links contributing to the interactive graph view that visualizes the relationships between notes.(no content)One of Obsidian's most powerful features is its ability to create backlinks, which allow you to link one note to another seamlessly. By typing `[[note name]]`, you can connect related ideas and build a web of interconnected notes. This approach encourages a non-linear, networked way of thinking, making it easier to discover relationships between concepts.

## 2025 Updates

On February 20, 2025, Obsidian announced that its commercial licenses were now completely optional, citing, "The Commercial license terms were confusing and added unnecessary complexity to our pricing" as the reason for the change.(no content)On May 21, 2025, Obsidian launched its Bases core plugin in early access version 1.9.0. This feature was later released to all Obsidian users on August 18, 2025 in version 1.9.10.(no content)As of November 29, 2025, Obsidian has plans on their roadmap for a Base search feature, a Kanban view type, and a calendar view type.(no content)As of December 04, 2025, there are 2,692 community plugins available for Obsidian.

### Plugin Ecosystem
Users may customize their Obsidian vaults by using plugins, which extend the software's functionality with additional features or integration with other tools. Obsidian differentiates between core plugins, which are released and maintained by the Obsidian team, and Community plugins are primarily developed by members of the Obsidian community under open source licenses and hosted on GitHub.

Here are the search results for "Evernote notebooks web clipper organization 2025":

## Evernote Web Clipper Organization Features

### Smart Filing and Notebook Organization

Smart filing automatically suggests a related notebook and/or tag for new web clips based on the similarity of your new clip to previous clips.(no content)Once you click Save clip, Web Clipper smart filing automatically stores both the contents and the URL of a web page into Evernote, as a note in your default notebook. If you'd prefer not to save to your default notebook, you can specify how you'd like to organize your clips before saving them to Evernote, including renaming the title and selecting a notebook where you want to save your web clip. Evernote attempts to predict the most likely notebook based on how you've organized notes in the past.

### Organization Options

You can tag what you save so your research, photos, ideas, and more are all easy to find using keywords. You can also add titles and save content to the notebook that makes sense.(no content)You can organize your clips before saving by adding them to an existing notebook (or creating a new one), adding tags for easy location via search or adding remarks (text notes) within a note.

### Web Clipper Settings

Default clip action options include Article, Simplified Article, Full Page, Bookmark, and Screenshot. You can also choose what the clipper should do after clipping, whether to show related Evernote content alongside web search results, and enable or disable the Save to Evernote button when viewing PDF pages.

### Multi-Account Support

Web Clipper lets you quickly switch between two Evernote accounts so it's easier to keep your clips organized.

### New Features

There is a new way to clip your favorite pages with Web Clipper. This version includes the powerful multi-select mode: when clipping a webpage you can now freely select multiple sections and combine them in a single note.

### Browser Availability

For Chrome, Safari, Firefox, and Edge: Click the elephant button in the toolbar to launch the Web Clipper, then click Settings at the bottom of the panel.

Verified Reviews, Pros & Cons | Capterra","url":"https://www.capterra.com/p/235184/Simplenote/reviews/"},{"title":"Simplenote Is All You Need for Note-Taking—Here's Why","url":"https://www.makeuseof.com/why-simplenote-is-all-you-need-for-note-taking/"},{"title":"Slant - Simplenote vs Google Calendar detailed comparison as of 2025","url":"https://www.slant.co/versus/2123/2599/~simplenote_vs_google-calendar"},{"title":"Best Minimalist Apps for Focus & Productivity in 2025","url":"https://techgenyz.com/best-minimalist-apps-for-focus-productivity/"}]

Based on my search, here's what I found about Simplenote's minimalist sync design philosophy in 2025:

## Simplenote's Design Philosophy

Simplenote's design philosophy centers around minimalism, which is evident in its clean and uncluttered interface. This minimalist design is not only aesthetically pleasing but also functional, as it removes distractions and allows users to focus solely on their content.

### Core Principles

Simplenote's strategy remains squarely on speed and universal accessibility. By keeping the core feature set intentionally lean, they deliver a consistently responsive experience that aligns with a "less is more" philosophy.(no content)The native apps across all platforms "embody the core philosophy of Simplenote: minimalism and focus on the note taking."

### Minimalist Interface

Simplenote takes a minimalist approach to its interface. There are no toolbars full of formatting options or extra features like notebooks to group notes. The entire desktop interface consists solely of a sidebar with your tags and trash filters, the list of existing notes with search, a button to add a new note and a simple view for looking at an existing note or writing a new one.(no content)The white background, simple typography, and absence of unnecessary elements create an environment that supports productivity and encourages creativity.

### Sync and Speed

Unlike alternatives (such as Evernote) that are laden with features, Simplenote is fast to use and sync. Reviews from the Verge, Lifehacker, and a variety of other sources all describe using the Simplenote apps as very fast. While it may not be as feature-rich as other apps, the responsiveness of the app and simple interface keep it easy to use while never slowing down the user when they need to quickly take a note.(no content)Simplenote offers effortless syncing between devices. Whether you're jotting down ideas on your laptop, refining them on your tablet, or reviewing them on your phone, your notes are always current. There's no need for manual syncing or worrying about outdated versions—everything updates in real time.(no content)The app supports offline access, allowing users to view and edit notes without an internet connection, with changes syncing once connectivity is restored.

### 2025 Minimalist App Context

In 2025, minimalist apps focus on doing one thing exceptionally well, then disappear without taking much attention. The goal is not to implement fewer features but to apply an intentional level of simplicity; a small, well-chosen stack removes friction and allows for real workflow.(no content)For individuals who need blazingly fast capture without bells and whistles, applications like Bear (for macOS and iOS users) and Simplenote (cross-platform and open source) offer uncluttered interfaces and consistent syncing.

### Background

Simplenote has been providing its minimalist note-taking experience since its founding in 2008. It's now owned by Automattic—the company behind WordPress.com—and is based in San Francisco.(no content)Simplenote is completely free. It was formerly offered with a premium option, but now all features are available at no cost, and there are no advertisements, maintaining a clean interface.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Roam Research as a networked thought and graph database tool in 2025:

## Roam Research Overview

Roam Research is as easy to use as a word document or bulleted list, and as powerful for finding, collecting, and connecting related ideas as a graph database.(no content)It allows users to collaborate with others in real time, or store all data locally.

## Key Features

Roam Research is a note-taking and knowledge management tool that organizes information in a non-linear, networked structure. Unlike traditional note apps that rely on folders and hierarchies, Roam is designed around bi-directional linking, enabling users to connect ideas in a way that mirrors how the human brain works.(no content)Roam operates on the principle of networked thought, enabling users to build a personalized knowledge graph where notes are interconnected through bi-directional links. This system promotes non-linear thinking and helps users discover new relationships between their ideas.(no content)Roam Research is a unique note-taking application known for its 'bidirectional linking' and graph database approach to knowledge management. Instead of traditional folders, users create 'daily notes' and link individual blocks of text, building a web of interconnected ideas that can be explored visually. This non-hierarchical structure encourages serendipitous discovery.

## 2025 Pricing & Status

As of December 2025, Roam Research offers pricing tiers including a Pro Plan that costs $15 per month when billed annually or $20 per month on a monthly basis.(no content)The lack of native mobile apps (as of 2025) limits the mobile experience, although the web version is mobile-friendly.

## Comparisons to Competitors

Roam Research is often compared with tools like Obsidian, Notion, Logseq, and Evernote. Compared to Obsidian, Roam is cloud-based and offers real-time collaboration, whereas Obsidian is local-first and more customizable through plugins. Notion offers databases, collaboration, and templates but lacks native bi-directional linking and graph-based navigation. Logseq is an open-source alternative with a similar interface and features, appealing to privacy-focused users.

## Target Users

Popular among researchers, writers, academics, and knowledge workers, Roam Research allows users to create a personal knowledge graph—a web of interlinked notes that grows more powerful as it's used. Its design encourages idea generation, long-term thinking, and deep work.

Instead of traditional folders, users create 'daily notes' and link individual blocks of text, building a web of interconnected ideas that can be explored visually. This non-hierarchical structure encourages serendipitous discovery.

## 2025 Pricing & Status

As of December 2025, Roam Research offers pricing tiers including a Pro Plan that costs $15 per month when billed annually or $20 per month on a monthly basis.(no content)The lack of native mobile apps (as of 2025) limits the mobile experience, although the web version is mobile-friendly.

## Comparisons to Competitors

Roam Research is often compared with tools like Obsidian, Notion, Logseq, and Evernote. Compared to Obsidian, Roam is cloud-based and offers real-time collaboration, whereas Obsidian is local-first and more customizable through plugins. Notion offers databases, collaboration, and templates but lacks native bi-directional linking and graph-based navigation. Logseq is an open-source alternative with a similar interface and features, appealing to privacy-focused users.

## Target Users

Popular among researchers, writers, academics, and knowledge workers, Roam Research allows users to create a personal knowledge graph—a web of interlinked notes that grows more powerful as it's used. Its design encourages idea generation, long-term thinking, and deep work.

Links: [{"title":"Logseq | Bellingcat's Online Investigation Toolkit","url":"https://bellingcat.gitbook.io/toolkit/more/all-tools/logseq"},{"title":"Logseq Product Information and Latest Updates (2025) | Product Hunt","url":"https://www.producthunt.com/products/logseq?launch=logseq"},{"title":"This Free Knowledge Base Tool Organizes My Thoughts Better Than Notion and Obsidian","url":"https://www.makeuseof.com/logseq-organizes-notes-better-than-notion-and-obsidian/"},{"title":"How to Get Started With Networked Thinking and Logseq","url":"https://blog.logseq.com/how-to-get-started-with-networked-thinking-and-logseq/"},{"title":"Logseq Reviews (2025) | Product Hunt","url":"https://www.producthunt.com/products/logseq/reviews"},{"title":"logseq/logseq | DeepWiki","url":"https://deepwiki.com/logseq/logseq"},{"title":"Logseq and the Rise of the Integrated Thinking Environment","url":"https://blog.logseq.com/logseq-and-the-rise-of-the-integrated-thinking-environment/"},{"title":"Why Logseq Drove Me Crazy but Changed How I Think (Here’s My Complicated Take) | by ItReviewHub | Aug, 2025 | Medium","url":"https://itreviewhub.medium.com/why-logseq-drove-me-crazy-but-changed-how-i-think-heres-my-complicated-take-c8fc48cbc9b2"},{"title":"Logseq Lady #4: Bi-Directional Linking | by Tansy Bradshaw | Logseq Lady | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/logseq-lady/bi-directional-linking-6ef8798ab7e7"},{"title":"Logseq: Local-first, privacy-focused outliner notebook | AlternativeTo","url":"https://alternativeto.net/software/logseq/about/"}]

Here's what I found about Logseq's outliner and bidirectional links features:

## Logseq: Outliner and Bidirectional Links Features

### Core Architecture

Logseq is a privacy-first, local-first knowledge management platform built on a block-based outliner model. Users create and organize knowledge in hierarchical blocks within pages, forming graphs that can be stored either as plain-text Markdown/Org files or in a structured database.(no content)All content in Logseq is stored as blocks - hierarchical units identified by UUID. Pages are blocks with :block/name, regular blocks have :block/parent linking to ancestors. This uniform model enables outliner operations, property attachment, and bidirectional linking.

### Key Outliner Features

Core capabilities include block‑based outliner notes, bidirectional links and graph view, tasks/flashcards, whiteboards, advanced (Datalog‑style) queries, PDF annotation, plugins/themes, and Zotero integration.(no content)Logseq adds another hierarchy to outlines, as it's a tool that's built on top of a graph database. That means that connections between text don't just exist on indentation level, but that any piece of information can connect with any other piece—regardless on what page that information lives.(no content)After months of daily use, what genuinely sets Logseq apart includes Block-Level Granularity: Every bullet point is addressable and linkable. You can reference specific thoughts from months ago without hunting through entire documents.

### Bidirectional Linking

Bi-directional links provide a simple yet powerful feature set to add relationships between different notes in your note database at both the macro (page) and micro (block or paragraph) levels. What makes bi-directional links so powerful is the creation of so-called 'back-links' in the page or block that is being referenced. When a link to a target is created at some source, another link is created in the target location, which points back to the original source. That means you can go to a page and see all the times that other pages have linked to it.(no content)What's particularly notable about bi-directional linking in Logseq is the way it sorts: while it creates a linked reference to the page, it also scans all other pages and creates an unlinked reference. It is these unlinked references that can add context to the original note or be the starting point for a whole new note.

### Comparison with Alternatives

Obsidian is a powerful Markdown-based tool, but Logseq natively supports outliner functionality without plugins, making it more streamlined for users needing hierarchical organization.(no content)Roam Research pioneered this space but comes with a hefty price tag and cloud dependency. Logseq delivers 90% of Roam's functionality for free, with better privacy guarantees.

### User Feedback (2025)

Logseq is highly praised for its open-source nature, local-first data storage, Markdown support, and privacy features. Users admire its capabilities for personal knowledge management (PKM) and note-taking with integrations like plugins, block referencing, and excellent task management. While appreciated for various features like bidirectional links and customizability, some users report bugginess in multi-device sync and slow development.(no content)The core app is free/open-source (AGPL-3.0-only). Optional Logseq Sync is offered to supporters and is transitioning from beta; Logseq Pro (sync with own storage, real-time collaboration) has been announced, but pricing is not finalized publicly.

## iA Writer: Minimalist Distraction-Free Writing in 2025

iA Writer is a premier writing tool designed for writers seeking a distraction-free, typewriter-like experience. With over 2 million users worldwide, this dedicated writing application offers a seamless markdown interface, making it a top choice(no content)for authors, bloggers, and content creators.

### Key Features

- **Distraction-Free Interface:** Focus solely on your writing with a clean, minimalist design.
- **Markdown Support:** Effortlessly format your text with markdown capabilities.
- **Powerful Editing Tools:** Enhance your writing with syntax highlighting and style check features.
- **Cross-Platform Compatibility:** Access your work on multiple devices, including Windows, macOS, iOS, and Android.
- **Cloud Integration:** Seamlessly sync your documents with popular cloud services.

### Focus Mode

iA Writer's Focus Mode is designed to help writers concentrate by highlighting only the sentence or paragraph they're working on, minimizing distractions and reducing cognitive overload.(no content)Once you have text on the screen, you can set iA Writer to focus only on the sentence or paragraph you're currently typing. The remainder of the text fades into the background. It's there, but fainter.

### User Experience

iA Writer has fewer features, by design. But each one is intentional, built for focus, and made to help you write better. Nothing distracts, nothing decorates.(no content)iA Writer is a markdown text editor, which allows it to ditch all of those buttons and settings for a truly distraction-free interface. Much like Ulysses, you can create documents without your fingers leaving the keyboard.

### Export Options

You can export documents in various formats, including PDF, Word, and HTML, catering to diverse needs.

### Availability

iA Writer offers a free trial for Mac or Windows. Write, edit, and export without limits for two weeks. No sign-up, no ads, no strings attached.(no content)iA Writer is available for Mac, Windows, iPhone, and iPad.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Joplin, the open source markdown note-taking application:

## Joplin - Open Source Note-Taking App

Joplin is a free, open source note taking and to-do application that can handle a large number of notes organized into notebooks. The notes are searchable, can be copied, tagged and modified either from the applications directly or from your own text editor. The notes are in Markdown format.

### Key Features

- **Cross-Platform Support**: The app is available on Windows, macOS, Linux, Android and iOS.(no content)A terminal app is also available!

- **Synchronization**: The notes can be securely synchronised using end-to-end encryption with various cloud services including Nextcloud, Dropbox, OneDrive and Joplin Cloud.

- **Offline-First**: Joplin is "offline first", which means you always have all your data on your phone or computer. This ensures that your notes are always accessible, whether you have an internet connection or not.

- **Security**: Uses End-To-End Encryption (E2EE) to secure your notes and ensure no-one but yourself can access them.

- **Extensibility**: Customise the app with plugins, custom themes and multiple text editors (Rich Text or Markdown). Or create your own scripts and plugins using the Extension API.

- **Web Clipper**: Use the web clipper extension, available on Chrome and Firefox, to save web pages or take screenshots as notes.

- **Import/Export**: Notes exported from Evernote can be imported into Joplin, including the formatted content (which is converted to Markdown), resources (images, attachments, etc.) and complete metadata (geolocation, updated time, created time, etc.). Plain Markdown files can also be imported.

### Latest Version (2025)

As of September 1, 2025, the latest version is Joplin 3.4.10.(no content)Recent improvements include disabled in-editor Markdown rendering by default, fixes for notifications, and OCR-related fixes.

### Alternatives

The best Joplin alternatives are Obsidian, Standard Notes and Logseq. There are more than 100 apps similar to Joplin for Mac, Windows, Linux, Android and more.

Joplin continues to be a popular choice for users seeking a privacy-focused, open-source alternative to commercial note-taking applications like Evernote or OneNote.

Based on my search results, here's what I found about Typora as a WYSIWYG markdown editor in 2025:

## Typora WYSIWYG Markdown Editor - 2025 Overview

### Core Design Philosophy

Instead of a split-pane view with code on one side and a preview on the other, Typora renders your Markdown syntax in real-time within a single, clean window.(no content)Typora remains the benchmark for minimal, distraction-free markdown writing. It pioneered the "what you see is what you mean" approach.

### Key Design Features

Typora is a minimalist, distraction-free Markdown editor that provides a seamless live preview, making it ideal for writers who need to focus on content creation without unnecessary distractions. Its real-time live preview instantly renders Markdown syntax as formatted text, featuring a minimalist interface with a clean, no-distraction writing environment with no sidebars or menus.(no content)Its main interface is WYSIWYG, with a dash of markdown syntax visibility. For example, when you focus the cursor in italic or bold text, you'll see the markers either side of that text.

### User Experience

It's not getting in your way. You write markdown as you do in every simple text editor. No short-cuts needed. When leaving the current line Typora renders your input. When re-entering the line, it displays the source. This even works for diagrams and formulae.

### Advanced Features (2025)

The editor's power extends beyond basic text with robust support for complex elements like tables, mathematical formulas using LaTeX, and even diagrams such as sequence charts and flowcharts. Its outline panel provides effortless navigation through long documents.(no content)It offers cross-platform support available for Windows, macOS, and Linux, custom themes with the ability to customize the visual style using CSS, and export options to easily export your work to PDF, HTML, Word, and other formats.

### 2025 Market Position

Choose Typora if you want the cleanest possible writing experience and don't need AI or collaboration features.(no content)Where it falls short: No AI integration. Limited collaboration. Feels isolated from modern development workflows.(no content)Typora offers an unmatched writing experience. It feels like writing in Word, but outputs clean Markdown. It's perfect for those who need both beautiful layout and technical structure.

Based on my search results, here's information about **Ulysses**, a writing-focused app with distraction-free features:

## Overview

Ulysses is a minimalist, distraction-free writing app that supports short and long-form projects. Bloggers, authors, and content creators use it to write articles, books, and more.(no content)It's strictly designed for Apple users writing on a Mac, iPad, or iPhone, which unfortunately rules out writers on Windows and Android.

## Distraction-Free Features

One of Ulysses' biggest strengths is its clean, uncluttered interface. Upon launching the app, you're greeted with a simple three-pane layout: The left pane houses your library, the middle pane displays a list of "sheets" (documents), and the right pane is your writing area. There are no distracting toolbars, rulers, complex menus or other interface elements vying for your attention—just a serene, text-focused space for you to write in.(no content)If you want to enter distraction-free mode, you can easily hide the library and sheet list with a single click. Everything about Ulysses' interface is designed to keep you focused on your words.

### Key Distraction-Free Tools:

- **Full-screen mode**, customizable themes, and typewriter scrolling. This minimalist design helps authors focus solely on writing without sidebar clutter or formatting menus.

- **Typewriter mode** whereby your current sentence remains in the center of the screen—useful if typing on a larger monitor.

- Writers can fine-tune Typewriter Mode to their personal needs, from highlighting paragraphs and sentences, to fixed scrolling and marking of the current line—for the most focused writing experience.

- You can hide all the menus on the left and only display the word processing screen so you can type away without getting distracted by all your other notes, lists, etc.

## Additional Features

- The app uses a customized version of Markdown for text formatting. With Markdown, you use simple characters to style text—for example, wrapping a word with asterisks to make it bold—which allows you to apply formatting without taking your hands off the keyboard or fiddling with menus.

- Writers can set word count goals per sheet, session, or entire group. Progress bars and target alerts provide motivational feedback during writing sprints or deadline-driven projects.

- The app includes built-in blog publishing, grammar and style checkers, and Shortcuts support.

## Pricing

Ulysses offers a 14-day trial, after which you will have to choose a monthly ($5.99) or yearly ($49.99) subscription.(no content)Ulysses has existed since 2003, and the developers constantly maintain and improve the app and adapt it to new technologies, making it a reliable companion for any writer.

Here are the search results for "Standard Notes encryption longevity privacy design 2025":

## Standard Notes: Encryption, Longevity & Privacy Design

### Encryption Architecture

The Standard Notes Protocol describes a set of procedures that ensure client-side encryption of data in such a way that makes it impossible for the server, which houses the data, to read or decrypt the data. It treats the server as a dumb data-store that simply saves and returns values on demand.(no content)All notes, tags, and other data generated using the Standard Notes applications are encrypted using XChaCha20-Poly1305, one of the strongest forms of encryption available (recommended by leading technology companies like Cloudflare and Google as a replacement to AES-256).(no content)Data is encrypted using keys generated from your password. When you choose your account password during registration, they use a password-stretching algorithm called Argon2 to strengthen your password and generate the necessary keys. When using Argon2 with a strong password, encryption keys become impossible to guess, even with a network of supercomputers.

### Key Management Design

Clients generate a long secret key by stretching a user-inputted password using a KDF. The first half of that key is kept locally as the "master key" and is never revealed to the server. The second half of that key is sent to the server as the "account server password."(no content)The master key is then used to encrypt an arbitrary number of items keys. Items keys are generated randomly and not based on the account password. Items keys are used to encrypt syncable data, like notes, tags, and user preferences. Items keys themselves are also synced to user accounts, and are encrypted directly with the master key.

### Security Update & Version Expiration

Older versions of their encryption specification are hardcoded to expire after a set time, usually about 1 year from the release of the upgraded version. After a version expires, it is no longer accepted without explicit consent from the user.(no content)In designing their architecture to make the server "dumb," they treat the server as a non-trustworthy entity because servers are not in the user's control, while the app in front of them is more directly in their control. They've designed the system to require a trustworthy application, but not a trustworthy server.

### Privacy Principles

All data—no matter if you're using a PC, a phone, or a tablet—is encrypted anytime it touches a disk, like on their servers or on your device, using a password no one in the world knows but you. Because this data is end-to-end encrypted, they can't read it, and they can't sell it. Even if someone forced their way into their data centers, your data would be complete gibberish to them.(no content)They engage with leading security researchers on a recurring basis to conduct independent audits of their cryptography, application, and server code.(no content)All code is open and available. They believe the definition of end-to-end encryption should mean that code on both ends is viewable by the public.

### Longevity Design

Standard Notes follows principles that maximize longevity, not growth.(no content)For long term storage, Standard Notes and Obsidian are the two contenders that make local and offline access a priority. Standard Notes takes a hybrid approach to cloud and offline access—data is seamlessly and securely synced to other devices, but a local copy is kept on your device so that your data is accessible offline.

### Device Security

Standard Notes' approach to security is on by default, so there are no settings users must proactively configure to attain the most secure experience possible.(no content)A passcode is a device-specific secret that can be configured to increase the level of protection on local devices. Passcode keys are never saved to disk, either in encrypted or unencrypted form, and are generated each time you start the application, residing in memory until you lock or quit the application.

Based on my search, here's what I found about note-taking app UX patterns, best practices, and user complaints in 2025:

## Key UX Best Practices for Note-Taking Apps in 2025

### Design Fundamentals
- Google Keep is cited as a good example, offering "a clean, intuitive interface that makes note-taking effortless."
- A common mistake is using inconsistent elements across the app, such as mismatched icons or different button styles. Consistency reduces the learning curve, letting users focus on what the app does rather than how to use it.
- Apps should follow platform-specific design patterns for familiarity (iOS vs. Android).

### 2025 Mobile/Web App UX Trends
- By 2025, it's no longer enough for an application to simply work—it must be seamless, intuitive, fast, and inclusive.
- Clear navigation, cross-platform consistency, and transparent data handling are no longer optional—they're expectations.
- Personalization has become integral to mobile app design, leveraging user data and AI to tailor content, layout, and features to each individual. Rather than every user seeing the same static interface, apps in 2025 often adjust what you see based on your behavior, preferences, location, or even the time of day.

### AI & Advanced Features
- Dynamic Content Prioritization means UI elements and content adapt based on user engagement patterns, past actions, and real-time context. Personalized Navigation means UI adapts to show frequently used features or content first. Predictive Interfaces have elements or actions appear proactively before the user explicitly seeks them.
- Continuity and integration are part of "seamless" UX: users might start a task on mobile and continue on another device. Cloud sync and handoff features help the journey feel smooth.

### User Complaints
- Regarding Notion, "The UX is sometimes hit or miss; the windows you'd want to open as separate pages often open as popups and vice-versa."
- For Obsidian, "it's great if you want to spend a lot of time on the tool itself. If you're looking for collaboration or integration features where you can push out information or tasks to support your productivity workflow—it's not built for that."

### Accessibility Requirements
- Designing for everyone, ensuring usability for people with diverse abilities and situational limitations, is a core ethical and legal requirement in 2025.
- Best practices include ensuring sufficient color contrast ratios (WCAG 2.1 AA/AAA), providing options for adjustable font sizes, properly labeling all interactive elements with alt text, and ensuring all app functionality is accessible via keyboard.

### Digital Wellbeing
- Apps should consider features that promote healthy screen time and minimize addictive patterns.

- Best practices include ensuring sufficient color contrast ratios (WCAG 2.1 AA/AAA), providing options for adjustable font sizes, properly labeling all interactive elements with alt text, and ensuring all app functionality is accessible via keyboard.

### Digital Wellbeing
- Apps should consider features that promote healthy screen time and minimize addictive patterns.

Here are the search results for note-taking app sidebar layout, split view, and navigation patterns in 2025:

## Note-Taking App Navigation Patterns

### Sidebar Layouts in Popular Apps

**Notion** uses a sidebar split into two sections: Teamspaces, which groups all the pages you share with the rest of your team, and Private, where you can have your own notes.

**Obsidian** offers the ability to sort notes into folders and subfolders using the sidebar like a normal notes app, but more interestingly, you can link between them using internal hyperlinks.(no content)In the sidebar for every note, you can see all the notes linking to the current note and all the notes it links out to.

**Google Keep** features a lightbulb icon in the right sidebar of Gmail. Click it, and you have quick access to all your Google Keep notes. That same sidebar is there in all Google Docs, Google Calendar, and Google Drive.

### Split View Navigation

**Notability** organizes with Subjects and Dividers, all visible from a left sidebar. Notes appear on the right in grid or list view, and it shines with multi-note split view, letting you place two notes side by side inside the app.

**Goodnotes** opens to a tidy grid or list of notebooks and folders. You can sort by name, date, or type, and organize with nested folders. Favorites and shared items live in the sidebar for quick access.

**NotePlan** allows opening any note in a split view on macOS by pressing control (⌃) and clicking on the note, or right clicking the note in the left sidebar and selecting "Open In Split View".

**Quiver** uses the classic three-pane layout: the left pane is the "Sidebar", the middle pane is the "Note List", and the right pane is the "Note View". You can easily switch between single pane, two panes or three panes layout using keyboard shortcuts.

### SwiftUI Navigation Patterns

For developers building note apps, the NavigationSplitView is a user interface component that enables a sidebar-detail interface, where the primary view (sidebar) controls the content of the secondary view (detail). This design pattern is particularly effective for applications that manage a collection of items and need to display detailed information about a selected item.(no content)In iOS 16 and macOS 13, NavigationSplitView in SwiftUI allows for defining multicolumn navigation and programmatically controlling visibility of the leading columns.

### 2025 Sidebar Design Best Practices

According to design experts, the sidebar remains one of the most reliable and effective tools for navigating complex interfaces. A well-designed sidebar menu can streamline user journeys and reinforce visual hierarchy.

Key characteristics of effective sidebar menus include:
- Vertical orientation with clean flow that aids scanning, expandable sections (accordions and dropdowns) for scalability, iconography for readability, responsiveness across screen sizes, context-aware design that adjusts based on page or user action, and clear call-to-actions.

The new & upcoming note-taking apps to… | by Francesco D'Alessio | Medium","url":"https://francescod.medium.com/6-new-note-taking-apps-in-2025-27d97de85e36"},{"title":"19 Best Note Taking Apps for Boosting Productivity in 2025","url":"https://thedigitalprojectmanager.com/tools/best-note-taking-apps/"},{"title":"Note taking App UX-UI Design | Figma","url":"https://www.figma.com/community/file/1272877363595791213/note-taking-app-ux-ui-design"},{"title":"Top 10 Apps for Seamless Note-Taking and Documentation Integration in 2025 - Serenity Blog","url":"https://www.serenity.coach/blog/best-note-taking-integrations-2025"}]

Here are the search results for your query about note-taking app search filters, sorting, organization, and UX in 2025:

## Search UX Best Practices

Implementing filters and sorting that match user goals, highlighting keywords in results, and testing different layouts maximizes clarity and usability.(no content)A good search experience anticipates user intent, handles typos gracefully, provides helpful suggestions, and presents results clearly. Every element from the placement of the search bar to filters, sorting options, and result highlights affects usability.

Key recommendations include:
- Making the search box easy to find, offering helpful autocomplete and suggestions, handling typos and fuzzy queries gracefully, showing relevant clear results, and providing filters and sorting options.
- 5-8 autocomplete suggestions are ideal—enough to be helpful without overwhelming users.

## Note-Taking App Organization Features

Note apps with organizational tools such as folders, tags, and powerful search functionalities help you easily categorize and retrieve notes. Bi-directional linking and dependencies are particularly valued features that help understand how work is connected and dependent on other priorities or ideas.

### Notion
Reviewers appreciate Notion's search/filter capabilities for quickly finding specific tasks, notes, or projects within a large workspace. They value the ability to search by keywords, tags, categories, and dates, and to filter and sort data in tables. Users report that the feature is fast and intuitive, though some feel it could be more user-friendly and offer more advanced options.

However, one criticism of Notion is the maze of organization where you're sometimes lost in the app, and the breadcrumbs aren't always helpful. The UX is sometimes hit or miss—windows you'd want to open as separate pages often open as popups and vice-versa.

### Obsidian
With Obsidian, you can sort your notes into folders and subfolders using the sidebar, but more interestingly, you can link between them using internal hyperlinks. Typing [[ brings up a dialog box that lets you select any other note to link to. In the sidebar for every note, you can see all the notes linking to the current note and all the notes it links out to. There's a Graph view that visualizes all these links too.

### Evernote
Users find Evernote's search/filter functionality extremely helpful for quickly locating information within a large volume of notes. They appreciate the ability to search by keywords, titles, dates, and even within images and attached documents. Reviewers highlight the speed and accuracy of the search results, noting that it saves time and improves organization. Some users mention the need for more detailed filtering options.

### UpNote
UpNote is a polished and user-friendly note-taking app designed for seamless organization and creative productivity. Its versatile structure allows you to categorize notes using hashtags, nested notebooks, pinned notes, and keyword filters for precise navigation.

## Key Criteria for Note-Taking Apps in 2025

The app must be fast and intuitive—if creating a new note requires more effort than scribbling on a business card, its adoption is doomed to fail. Editing, sorting, and searching must be fluid and natural, without wrestling with a complex interface.(no content)Smart organization tools like folders, tags, and sorting options should adapt to the user's way of studying. Interactive features like handwriting recognition, AI summaries, or real-time collaboration elevate workflow. A clutter-free, intuitive interface prevents frustration and boosts productivity.(no content)Tagging and organization helps users group, filter, and retrieve notes using folders, tags, or labels. Cross-device sync ensures notes are always up to date and accessible across desktop, mobile, and web.

Based on the search results, here's a comparison of block-based editors vs document editors for note-taking in 2025:

## Block-Based Editors vs Document Editors for Note-Taking (2025)

### What Are Block-Based Editors?

Block-based editors allow users to break down their notes into individual blocks of text, images, links, and more, making it easy to rearrange, delete, and modify content as needed.(no content)While some software offers brilliant features that can be a struggle to use effectively, block-based editors offer a better way to edit your content fast with minimal fuss.

### Top Block-Based Editors

**Notion**
Notion seamlessly blends note-taking, project management, and collaboration into one platform. With 62% of Fortune 100 companies using it and being named the #1 knowledge base on G2 for three consecutive years, Notion has established itself as a top choice for teams aiming to streamline productivity.(no content)Notion's block-based approach lets users turn every piece of content into a movable, customizable element. This flexibility allows for creating anything from basic notes to intricate databases, all within the same interface.

**Obsidian**
Obsidian is a free application that functions as both a markdown editor and knowledge management tool. At its core, you can create, edit, and preview markdown files, but the true power lies in how you can use these files to form an interconnected network of notes.(no content)Unlike other note-taking apps, Obsidian takes a minimalist approach. Instead of pushing a final product tailored to a specific way of thinking, it provides a streamlined foundation that's highly customizable.

**Roam Research**
Roam Research is a block-based editor designed for research and knowledge management. Its unique feature is the ability to create bi-directional links between blocks, allowing users to easily connect related information across their notes. Roam Research also offers features like backlinks, daily notes, and graph view, making it easy to navigate and explore the connections between different ideas.

**Coda**
Coda is a similar note-taking app to Notion. However, it has more advanced project management tools and also a user-friendlier app with a shorter learning curve.(no content)In Coda, you use blocks to organize and structure your content. It allows using different paragraph styles and adds quotes, callouts, and more. Similar to Notion, you can create blocks if you click on the "/" tab. When creating them, you can choose from many formats, such as text, tables, images, calendars, etc.

### Traditional Document Editors

Dropbox Paper is a minimalist document editor and collaboration tool connected with Dropbox's cloud storage. Its streamlined interface and collaboration features make it popular for team-based document editing and info-sharing.(no content)Evernote is one of the oldest names in the note-taking game. After a period of stagnation, new owners Bending Spoons took over in 2023, completely reworking the app's foundation. Now, in 2025, Evernote isn't just a note-taking app—it's trying to be a full productivity hub with tasks, calendar integration, and AI-powered features.

### Key Differences

| Feature | Block-Based Editors | Document Editors |
|---------|---------------------|------------------|
| Content Structure | Modular blocks | Linear flow |
| Reorganization | Drag-and-drop blocks | Copy/paste |
| Flexibility | High customization | Traditional formatting |
| Learning Curve | Steeper | Lower |

### Market Trends

The note-taking app market is on a sharp upward trajectory, expected to grow from $576 million in 2023 to $2.26 billion by 2032, with a compound annual growth rate of 16.4%.(no content)For primary choice, Notion is recommended for its versatility and collaboration features; OneNote as a budget option for reliable free functionality; and Obsidian for research-focused work building knowledge connections. Most major platforms are incorporating AI assistants for writing, summarization, and content generation.

### Recommendations by Use Case

For independent authors, the best note-taking app depends heavily on writing habits, research needs, and preferred creative methods. Nebo shines for those who prefer the tactile feel of handwriting and sketching ideas with a stylus. Evernote is an excellent choice for writers who need to clip and organize web-based research. Notion offers unmatched versatility for plot development and visual storytelling. Obsidian is ideal for those who love interlinking complex story components or keeping a personal knowledge base.

Here are the search results for note-taking app design patterns related to empty states and quick capture in 2025:

## Empty States Design Patterns

When designing empty states, users would often land on feature pages that initially had no results, so leaving them with a blank screen was out of the question. Every empty state should be crafted to serve a purpose - a short, focused message explaining why the page was empty and what action the user could take, with clear CTA buttons making it easy to follow through.(no content)Empty states are designed to nudge users toward an action that will populate the screen. For example, an empty task manager might say "You have no tasks yet" and include a bright, friendly "Add your first task" button.

### Types of Empty States

Not all empty states serve the same purpose. Some are meant to reassure, others are there to push users toward action, and some exist purely to celebrate a job well done. They can be broken into three main types.(no content)Don't treat empty states like ad-hoc screens. When building a component library, include empty state patterns in your design system. Use illustrations or icons to fit your brand tone, though if an image doesn't help explain the moment or guide the action, it's just decoration. Put headline first ("No results found"), then secondary explanation ("Try searching for something else"), and CTA ("Browse categories").(no content)Educational content that helps users get oriented and started is another option for turning an otherwise empty space into a positive and productive experience for first time users. A good rule of thumb is that a primary resource on a page could benefit from a more educational approach, while basic empty states may suffice for secondary resources.(no content)Another strategy for addressing an otherwise empty space is to provide pre-built content that allows new users to get started quickly. Starter content can provide the opportunity for users to dive in and learn about primary features with sample data. Users can tinker, examining and deleting content without serious consequences.

## Quick Capture Patterns in Note-Taking Apps

Each note taking app lets users cut straight to the chase to add a note through a call to action button on the home screen that leads to a plain screen displaying typed text in big, bold letters.(no content)ClickUp's Notepad is designed for quick note-taking, whether on phone or desktop, serving as a reliable option for capturing thoughts on the go.(no content)Google Keep allows users to effortlessly capture a variety of content, including notes, lists, photos, and audio. The voice note feature is particularly useful - when in a rush and needing to jot down something important, users can simply record a voice memo and transcribe it into text later.(no content)Simplenote is designed for users who value speed and simplicity in note-taking. Its minimalist interface keeps distractions at bay, allowing for quick note entry and easy access. Syncing is seamless across devices, ensuring ideas are always at your fingertips.(no content)Google Keep's primary strength lies in its simplicity and the ability to quickly capture thoughts, to-do lists, and even visual information across all devices. The interface is designed for speed, allowing users to jot down ideas the moment inspiration strikes.

## Visual & Connected Note-Taking

Napkin wants to capture ideas, bring them back to life & connect with others. The concept is to bring your ideas, connect them, and clip good ideas from Chrome or iOS. Then, Napkin will curate a list of ideas for you.(no content)Obsidian is ideal for users who want local, markdown-based notes in a customizable, link-rich format. Obsidian organizes notes in "vaults", creating a powerful, interconnected knowledge graph.

### Key Design Considerations

Landing on a new experience might cause a dazed feeling from trying to orient oneself amongst the nav bars, icons, and structure of information. Hard landings can be softened by a delightful onboarding flow that catches users and glides them along the main details users need to know. This onboarding experience can be a critical factor for users understanding the value of the app.

Here are the search results for note-taking app design patterns related to empty states and quick capture in 2025:

## Empty States Design Patterns

When designing empty states, users would often land on feature pages that initially had no results, so leaving them with a blank screen was out of the question. Every empty state should be crafted to serve a purpose - a short, focused message explaining why the page was empty and what action the user could take, with clear CTA buttons making it easy to follow through.(no content)Empty states are designed to nudge users toward an action that will populate the screen. For example, an empty task manager might say "You have no tasks yet" and include a bright, friendly "Add your first task" button.

### Types of Empty States

Not all empty states serve the same purpose. Some are meant to reassure, others are there to push users toward action, and some exist purely to celebrate a job well done. They can be broken into three main types.(no content)Don't treat empty states like ad-hoc screens. When building a component library, include empty state patterns in your design system. Use illustrations or icons to fit your brand tone, though if an image doesn't help explain the moment or guide the action, it's just decoration. Put headline first ("No results found"), then secondary explanation ("Try searching for something else"), and CTA ("Browse categories").(no content)Educational content that helps users get oriented and started is another option for turning an otherwise empty space into a positive and productive experience for first time users. A good rule of thumb is that a primary resource on a page could benefit from a more educational approach, while basic empty states may suffice for secondary resources.(no content)Another strategy for addressing an otherwise empty space is to provide pre-built content that allows new users to get started quickly. Starter content can provide the opportunity for users to dive in and learn about primary features with sample data. Users can tinker, examining and deleting content without serious consequences.

## Quick Capture Patterns in Note-Taking Apps

Syncing is seamless across devices, ensuring ideas are always at your fingertips.(no content)Google Keep's primary strength lies in its simplicity and the ability to quickly capture thoughts, to-do lists, and even visual information across all devices.    953→## 15. Strategic Frameworks & Influences 💡
   954→
   955→### 15.1 Rob Dyrdek's "Machine Mindset" & Existence Platform 💡 P2 - 2027
   956→**Philosophy:** Design time, don't just manage it. Life is a system to optimize.
   957→
   958→**Platform Reference:** Rob Dyrdek's **Existence** (launched November 2025)
   959→- "Time intelligence platform" that implements the Machine Mindset philosophy
   960→- Tags activities as Life/Work/Health/Sleep with time block tracking
   961→- Uncovers "hidden patterns shaping your life" through qualitative tracking
   962→- **Market Validation:** Major celebrity entrepreneur just launched exactly what we're building!
   963→
   964→**Core Mechanics (from Machine Mindset/Existence):**
   965→- **Time Blocking:** Every hour has a purpose (integrate with Calendar)
   966→- **Zero-Based Time Budgeting:** Allocate every minute like a dollar
   967→- **Qualitative Tracking:** Daily 0-10 ratings for Life, Work, Health (5+ years of data)
   968→- **Life Rhythms:** Weekly/monthly/quarterly review cadence
   969→- **"Rhythm of Existence" Document:** Guide covering time, health, money, family/work integration
   970→
   971→**Implementation Ideas for Neuman Brain:**
   972→- 💡 Daily rating prompts (Life/Work/Health 0-10) - inspired by Existence
   973→- 💡 Qualitative trend visualization (line charts over time)
   974→- 💡 Time block templates (morning routine, deep work, etc.)
   975→- 💡 Weekly reflection prompts
   976→- 💡 Pattern detection across Life/Work/Health categories
   977→
   978→**Our Differentiator:** Local-first privacy (vs Existence's likely cloud-based approach)
   979→
   980→### 15.2 PMP & ITIL for Personal Life 💡 P2 - 2027
   981→**Philosophy:** Enterprise-grade rigor applied to life management
   982→
   983→**PMP (Project Management Professional) Application:**
   984→- 💡 "Life Projects" module (major goals as projects)
   985→  - Project Charter (scope, timeline, budget)
   986→  - Stakeholders (who's involved in this life goal?)
   987→  - Critical Path (dependencies between life tasks)
   988→- 💡 Example: "Buy a house" project with tasks, milestones, deadlines
   989→
   990→**ITIL (Service Management) Application:**
   991→- 💡 Personal Service Level Agreements (SLAs)
   992→  - "I agree to workout 3x/week" → track adherence
   993→  - "Service Review" triggers on SLA breach
   994→- 💡 Continuous Improvement loops
   995→  - Retrospectives on failed habits
   996→  - Root cause analysis for missed goals

  1200→## Appendix: Feature Parity Methodology
  1201→
  1202→### How Parity Percentages Are Calculated
  1203→
  1204→**Objective:** Provide transparent, verifiable parity calculations to set accurate user expectations.
  1205→
  1206→**Formula:**
  1207→```
  1208→Parity % = (Shipped Features / Competitor Baseline Features) × 100
  1209→```
  1210→
  1211→**Competitor Baseline:** Count of essential features in the competitor's core product tier (not enterprise-only features).
  1212→
  1213→---

  1372→**Data Management (10/10 shipped):**
